_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Elbow Joint replacement is performed when other interventions, medical and surgical, will not offer a satisfactory outcome.
Underlying pathology includes: Osteoarthritis, Rheumatoid arthritis (RA).
Underlying pathology includes: Complex fracture of the elbow, even in the elderly.
Underlying pathology includes: Severely damaged or torn soft tissues in the elbow, resulting in instability.
Underlying pathology includes: Malignancy in or around the elbow.
Underlying pathology includes: Poor results from previous elbow surgery.
Severe pain with radiological changes of joint destruction in the presence of failed conservative treatment, or the quality of the bone is not suitable for fixation.
Elderly patients with low, comminuted distal humerus fractures.
The salvage of distal humerus nonunion, Ankylosis.

preauth.label: X-ray/ CT labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)- affected limb justifying the surgery

claim.label: Post op X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)-affected limb, showing the implant

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post-op X-ray / CT of Elbow show the presence of implant?

common_queries: Pre-operative X-ray/CT of affected limb not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or does not justify the surgery.
Post-operative X-ray/CT of affected limb not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or does not show the implant.
Invoice or barcode of the implant is missing or illegible.
Clinical notes are incomplete or do not support the necessity of the procedure.

clinical_key_pointers: Outcomes of an Elbow Replacement: Replaces the damaged joint surfaces with a synthetic joint.
Outcomes of an Elbow Replacement: A pain free, mobile elbow that enables patient to perform normal everyday activities.
This procedure may be associated with complications: Infection, Extensor mechanism dysfunction, Periprosthetic fractures, Wear, loosening, and osteolysis.
Fortunately, revision techniques developed over the last few years allow successful treatment of some of these complications.
